title: SyncGuard via __sync_guard_marker is a naming convention dressed as a type interface
description: SyncGuard is implemented as: any type with 'pub const __sync_guard_marker = {}' is a sync guard. Any struct that happens to have this field — including by accident — becomes a sync guard for linter purposes. There is no verification that the type actually holds a resource until dropped, or that it represents a lock. This is a naming convention, not a type contract. The double-underscore prefix is a code smell (reserved-name-style hacks). A proper design would be a pub interface 'SyncGuard' with a destructor requirement, not an ad-hoc marker field. The linter cannot be confident it has caught all sync guards, only the ones that declared the marker.
